channel.title: Endnotes
channel.description: Recent articles and dossiers from Endnotes (endnotes.org.uk)

item.title: Preface to the German edition of The Return of Inflation
item.author: Paul Mattick
item.description: An obvious danger in writing a book about a phenomenon of current interest is that said phenomenon may develop in unexpected ways while the book is being edited, printed, and distributed. I worried about this when I started to work on The Return of Inflation three years ago. What, for instance, if “

item.title: Prolegomena on the ‘system of states’
item.author: Robert Ferro, Raffaele Sciortino
item.description: It is generally acknowledged that Karl Marx envisaged a volume dedicated to the state in the plan of Capital — a volume of which he did not even write a draft. After Marx, several scholars have insisted on the incompleteness of Marxian theory in this respect, and while none has set him- or herself t

item.title: Communism: Social Love?
item.author: Howard Slater
item.description: Prefatory NoteSick with revolution, mad with loveGherasim LucaThe text below is a follow-through from Endnotes' Camatte dossier, not so much in terms of an overall commentary, but in terms of riding out along a tangent that seems to be only rarely discussed by communists or, when it is, it is relega

item.title: An extract from the Book of Abolitions
item.author: Karl Korsch
item.description: Translator’s note: The Book of Abolitions [Buch der Abschaffungen] is Korsch's final manuscript, composed in the United States in the 1950s.1 This translation was prepared with reference to the original German typescript held at the International Institute of Social History in Amsterdam, as well as

item.title: Mapping the Margins of Capitalism
item.author: Daniel Colligan
item.description: “Will I be able to convince all my readers? Hardly. But at least I have found one unparalleled advantage in this dialectical approach: it has enabled me, by taking a new, and somewhat more peaceful route, to avoid and by-pass the passionate disputes which the explosive word capitalism always arouses

item.title: The Least Cynical Man in the World
item.author: Luhuna Carvalho
item.description: Upon Ben Morea’s death, my first impulse was to recount his legend—a cinematic, almost implausible succession of adventures, a counter-history of postwar America, a long ode to the indomitable spirit of certain souls. The first draft of this text attempted that song, written from memory, a euphoric

item.title: Afterword to the French Edition of The Future of Revolution
item.author: Jasper Bernes
item.description: From Jasper Bernes, L’avenir de la révolution. Horizons communistes, de la Commune de Paris au soulèvement George Floyd (La Tempête 2026)

item.title: Lacuna Beach
item.author: Seb B. Grossmann
item.description: How are we to organize and bring about communism when proletarian struggle is increasingly located outside the sphere of production, and when production itself is dispersed across global logistics chains? It may seem paradoxical, in this context, to insist on the revolutionary potential of an idea f

item.title: Writings from the Wasteland
item.author: Gerardo Muñoz, Rodrigo Karmy Bolton, Idris Robinson
item.description: To mark the publication of Idris Robinson’s The Revolt Eclipses Whatever the World Has to Offer (Semiotext(e) 2025) we below publish translations of the accompanying texts of the book’s Spanish edition, Escritos desde la tierra baldía [Writings from the Wasteland]1  along with a recen

item.title: Angels And Demons
item.author: HB
item.description: For years, the local news in Los Angeles, KTLA, has been covering what it calls “the retail theft epidemic in Los Angeles.” On any given night of the week, any Los Angeles resident with a television set can flip to the local news station and watch headline after headline reporting on mass

item.title: Dossier: that summer feeling
item.description: The George Floyd Protests and America's Hot Pandemic Summer of 2020
